Scope: generated ToC/smoke source and main-path construction
Allowed action: Future generated ToC/smoke phases must consume or extend the existing source/main-path seam at its natural layer.
Blocked duplication: No second smoke-test generator, generated carrier source, or parallel main-path constructor under a phase-local module name.
Evidence: GeneratedSource exposes regular/variable smoke carriers and SM0 ledger; GeneratedMainPath exposes generatedMainPath, regular/variable paths, pair ledger, and no-CutSpec/no-matrix-data boundary status.
Future consumers: Phase 5 experiment request/output, O07 generic finite computation-test matrix, later generated arithmetic source phases.

Allowed action: Future consumers may consume the proof-carrying generated chain and add natural source-layer extensions when a missing structure is exposed.
Blocked duplication: No independent Schur/DtN/arithmetic generator that recomputes the same provenance chain outside the Generated* mainline.
Evidence: The robust semantic mainline graph and generated module chain already route source -> boundary/interior -> Schur/DtN -> arithmetic/charpoly/discriminant objects.
Future consumers: Phase 3/4 boundary-operator profiles, Phase 5 computation requests, O05/O06/O07/O08 operational arithmetic checks.

Future consumers: AR/action-format phases, later CM/CD/action comparison gates.

Evidence: generate_tables.py validates the single master and renders object, phase, scratchpad, module manifest, proof documentation, and reuse ledger views.
Future consumers: All planning/documentation phases, implementation prompts, public API classification.

Scope: semantic module consolidation and compatibility-preserving code compactness
Existing canonical seam: LEG10 module manifest; LEG11 reuse/duplication ledger; LEG5 declaration consumption classification; repo_inventory/raw_export import/declaration graph; old Lean module import paths as compatibility seams
Allowed action: Unify semantically identical schema/wrapper families only through a recorded LEG36 candidate row, old-module facades, declaration/consumer diff, and local build evidence. Prefer canonical schema modules at the natural producer layer.
Blocked duplication: No continued proliferation of unrelated clone wrappers once a canonical schema is validated; no aggressive merge of proof-stage chains, generated SM phase modules, or Cayley-Dickson S6 stages without provenance-preservation proof.
Evidence: V1.12 code spot-check: BoundarySource substrate ledger clone family; Handoff A_to_C/D/E alias family; Handoff B/C/D/E_to_A feedback-input family; no-merge warning for Smoke/SM and Cayley-Dickson proof chains.
